author: deme1744
content:
For EIP-8025, when I was starting to implement the first parts for this in Nimbus a month ago, I realized that there were actually only two parts that required a hardfork, `GetMetaData v4` and the addition of verifying the proof in `process_execution_payload`. In this initial implementation work, I did not yet create a specific fork for EIP-8025 but piggybacked on existing (or the next) fork (for now).

I wasn't sure however if the idea was to be able to enable optional-proofs fork independent or not. And thus was planning on adding a specific fork until it got added to an actual planned fork.
But now there is this PR (https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5014) which removes the updated `GetMetaData` call, and does so from what I understand with the idea of not being hardfork dependant.

Should we also rewrite `process_execution_payload` to have `proof_engine.verify_new_payload_request_header(new_payload_request_header)` behind some `optional-proofs-enabled` conditional or something similar?
